skill.name: testing
skill.description: Automated test generation, review, and execution for pytest-based projects.
skill.summary: Automated test generation, review, and execution for pytest-based projects.
skill.author: ByronWilliamsCPA

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged multiple markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The only confirmed external command guidance is a legitimate pytest command block, but running tests and mutation tests can execute project code. The weak cryptography alert at SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive; no cryptographic algorithm appears there.

medium_findings.title: External Test Commands May Execute Project Code
medium_findings.description: The skill documents commands such as pytest, coverage, mutation testing, and Hypothesis runs. These are legitimate for a testing skill, but executing tests in an untrusted repository can run arbitrary project code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command block is clearly present and semantically tied to test execution. It appears legitimate, but the execution of repository tests is still an elevated operational risk.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Content
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alert at SKILL.md line 3 is not supported by the file content. Later external command alerts point to markdown fences, a directory tree, or Python pytest examples rather than shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain a plain description, a test directory tree, and Python examples. No weak cryptographic algorithm or shell backtick execution appears in those lines.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Shell Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell commands for running pytest, coverage, mutation tests, and property-based test statistics. Users should review commands before running them in untrusted projects.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit in a bash code block. The risk depends on the target project and does not show malicious intent in the skill itself.

content.value_statement: Python teams need useful tests without losing time on repetitive setup. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through pytest generation, review, coverage, and execution workflows.
actual_capabilities: Generates pytest test cases for Python code.
Reviews existing tests for quality and coverage gaps.
Documents pytest commands for coverage, markers, verbosity, and Hypothesis statistics.
Organizes tests into unit, integration, end-to-end, security, and performance folders.
Promotes the arrange, act, assert pattern and shared fixtures.
limitations: It does not include separate workflow files referenced in the overview.
It does not guarantee that generated tests match every project architecture.
Running tests can execute project code and should be reviewed first.

use_cases.title: Add Unit Tests
use_cases.description: Create focused pytest cases for new Python functions using clear arrange, act, and assert sections.

use_cases.title: Review Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Check an existing test suite for missing cases, unclear fixtures, and coverage risks.

use_cases.title: Plan Quality Gates
use_cases.description: Define commands and folder structure for coverage, integration tests, performance tests, and security-focused tests.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate pytest tests for this Python function. Use arrange, act, and assert sections and include normal and edge cases.

prompt_templates.title: Review Existing Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these pytest tests for coverage gaps, weak assertions, fixture problems, and unclear organization.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Coverage Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pytest coverage plan for this package. Include unit, integration, security, and performance test areas.

prompt_templates.title: Design Full Test Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a pytest workflow for this repository with markers, coverage commands, mutation testing, and reporting steps.

output_examples.input: Generate tests for a price calculation function with discounts and invalid quantities.
output_examples.output: A concise pytest test plan covering valid prices, zero discounts, maximum discounts, invalid quantities, and rounding behavior.

output_examples.input: Review this existing test file for missing scenarios.
output: Missing boundary tests for empty input.
Assertions verify only status, not returned data.
A shared fixture would reduce duplicated setup.

output_examples.input: Create a coverage workflow for a Python package.
output_examples.output: A structured workflow with unit markers, integration markers, coverage reporting, mutation testing, and a minimum coverage target.

best_practices: Review test commands before running them in untrusted repositories.
Keep unit tests fast, isolated, and focused on one behavior.
Use coverage reports to guide test improvements, not as the only quality measure.
anti_patterns: Do not run generated test commands without checking project dependencies.
Do not replace meaningful assertions with snapshot-only checks.
Do not mix slow integration tests into the default unit test path.

faq.question: What testing framework does this skill target?
Can it help with coverage reports?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents pytest coverage commands and an 80 percent minimum coverage standard.

faq.question: Does it support integration and end-to-end tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes separate areas for integration, end-to-end, security, and performance tests.

faq.question: Does it execute tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides command guidance. Users should approve and run commands in their own environment.

faq.question: Is this safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. Running pytest or mutation tests can execute code from the repository.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
